description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone block with a predominantly light beige to off-white coloration, featuring natural striations and weathering marks across its surfaces. The block has a roughly cuboidal form but with uneven, jagged edges and corners, giving it a rugged, organic appearance. Subtle patches of moss or lichen are visible near the base, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The top surface is relatively flat with prominent dark streaks and some minor surface pitting, while the sides show more pronounced textural variation and discoloration. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its overall look suggests it could serve as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban or landscaped environment.
description: A large, rugged, light-colored stone block with natural striations, weathering, and some moss at the base. Its irregular, jagged form and flat top make it suitable as a barrier or obstacle in outdoor settings.
description_view_0: The top of the stone block is visible, showing a flat surface with dark streaks and minor pitting.
description_view_1: This angled side view highlights the block's sloped surface and rough, jagged edge, with some discoloration and texture detail.
description_view_2: The side appears more vertical and shows pronounced weathering, with subtle moss or lichen growth near the base.
description_view_3: This view reveals a rounded, irregular side with visible surface roughness and some white discoloration.
attribute_installation_method: set directly on compacted soil or sand
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible weathering with dark streaks, surface pitting, and some moss or lichen growth near the base, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure.
